name: Natural gas combustion; internal combustion engines, commercial/institutional, natural gas, turbine:cogeneration,pre-combustion chamber; at turbine
description: This unit process represents the combustion of natural gas fuel in the specified equipment. The data was developed from National Energy and Technology Laboratory (NETL) datasets, which incorporate Webfire circa 2014 as the primry source of emissions factors. For emissions control technologies-- SCR refers to selective catalytic reduction, SNCR refers to selective non-catalytic reduction. Boilers that are labeled "post-NSPS" are referring to the U.S. EPA New Source Performance Standards.

processDocumentation.timeDescription: Data best represents 2012. Validity date range is based on an assumption of a 10 year equipment life span.
processDocumentation.technologyDescription: Data represents combustion of natural gas fuel in the specified equipment. Commercial/institutional refers to equipment used in commercial establishments or institutional establishments such as medical centers, nursing homes, research centers, institutions of higher education, elementary and secondary schools, libraries, religious establishments, governmental buildings, hotels, restaurants, and laundries to provide electricity, steam, and/or hot water.
processDocumentation.completenessDescription: Relevant emissions to air captured, other flows such as ash or waste products from emissions control technologies are not included.
processDocumentation.dataSelectionDescription: NETL calculates combustion emissions based on the data reported in the EPA WebFIRE database. WebFIRE is the EPA's online database that contains emissions factors for criteria and hazardous air pollutants (HAP) for industrial and non-industrial processes and multiple reports submitted to the EPA using the Compliance and Emissions Data Reporting Interface (CEDRI) in response to regulatory requirements under Parts 60 and 63 of Title 40 of the U.S. Code of Federal Regulations (CFR).
processDocumentation.dataTreatmentDescription: NETL calculates combustion emissions based on the data reported in the EPA WebFIRE database. The database was filtered down to the intended fuel and equipment type, and emissions were normalized to the basis of 1 kg fuel combusted. Note, errors in the source dataset (e.g. unit conversion, typos, flow overlap) were identified and corrected.
processDocumentation.samplingDescription: For information on how data were collected, see Publication.
processDocumentation.dataCollectionDescription: Emissions data were collected from the EPA WebFIRE database.

sources.name: U.S. EPA (1999) Compilation of Air Pollutant Emission Factors
Battye, R. et al. (1994) Development and Selection of Ammonia Emission Factors Final Report
U.S. EPA (2011) Emission factors for greenhouse gas inventories

reviews.details: ERG internally reviewed the programs, underlying assumptions, and mathematical operations required to produce the JSON-LD files for addition to USLCI.

processDocumentation.intendedApplication: Situation C1 - Accounting, with system-external interactions - The intended application is a purely descriptive accounting / documentation of the analyzed system including existing interactions with other systems in the LCI model. Relevant emissions to air captured, other flows such as ash or waste products from emissions control technologies are not included. The target audience of this model includes LCA practitioners, industry, and the general public.
processDocumentation.projectDescription: This dataset was developed by the Federal LCA Commons Data Curator Eastern Research Group, Inc. (ERG) under subcontract (SUB-2024-10066) to the National Renewable Energy Laboratory under Department of Energy (DOE) Prime Contract No. DE-AC36-08GO28308. The work is funded by DOE's Advanced Materials & Manufacturing Technologies Office (AMMTO).
